pi_items.source: Script analysis (0.31 reduction) and web search (Genant criteria)

privileged_info_summary: The dataset is synthetically generated and simulates a thoracolumbar spine CT scan. The target vertebra for assessment is L1, which features a simulated compression fracture with a 31% height reduction. This reduction corresponds to a Genant Grade 2 (moderate) fracture, as the Genant classification defines Grade 2 as a 25% to 40% reduction in vertebral height. The physiological range for vertebral heights typically falls between approximately 11 mm and 30 mm.
